proposals.fieldLabel: Claude Opus 4.6 capability and context window stat
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Opus 4.6 — 1M token context window with 128K output capacity, enabling sustained multi-step reasoning and long-horizon autonomy
proposals.reason: The 5x context expansion and output capacity significantly changes the cost/capability tradeoff framework and selection criteria. This update reflects the current model capabilities as of March 2026 and directly impacts when to recommend Opus 4.6 over other models.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 1 — Update model family context windows and capability tradeoffs: Claude Opus 4.6 now has 1M token context (5x expansion) and 128K output capacity, requiring re-framing of cost/capability selection framework around sustained multi-step reasoning and long-horizon autonomy

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on adaptive thinking and context compaction
proposals.proposedValue: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking—a shift from fixed context budgets to dynamic reasoning depth. Instead of allocating a static amount of your context window to reasoning, Claude now adjusts thinking depth based on task complexity, allowing longer sessions with better resource management through context compaction strategies.
proposals.reason: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking and context compaction as core features that fundamentally change how context management works. This lesson addresses the shift from fixed budget allocation to dynamic reasoning depth, which is a significant evolution in the context management paradigm covered by Module 4.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 4 — Add adaptive thinking and context compaction strategies: Claude 4.6 introduces adaptive thinking (dynamic reasoning depth, not binary) and context compaction for longer sessions, shifting context management from fixed budget allocation to dynamic reasoning depth

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson on Structured Outputs with Strict Mode
proposals.proposedValue: For production systems that require guaranteed schema compliance, strict mode uses constrained decoding to ensure Claude's output always matches your JSON schema exactly. Strict mode (available 2026+) is production-ready and eliminates the need for retry logic or validation wrappers around structured outputs.
proposals.reason: Action item requests adding a comprehensive section on strict mode (production-ready 2026) with subsections on strict mode tool use, schema design best practices, and decision framework. This is substantial new content not present in current Module 5.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 5 — Add structured outputs with strict mode section: Strict mode (production-ready 2026) guarantees schema-compliant JSON via constrained decoding; add subsection on strict mode tool use, schema design best practices, and when to use strict: true vs JSON mode

proposals.fieldLabel: New lesson: Claude Code 2026 feature updates
proposals.proposedValue: New lesson covering auto-plan mode, auto-memory learning, voice input (20 languages), /claude-api skill, MCP elicitation dialogs, /effort command, InstructionsLoaded hooks, ExitWorktree tool, and updated permission auto-approval allowlist
proposals.reason: Module 3 needs to reflect the 2026 Claude Code feature releases documented in the research. These are substantial, production-ready features that significantly impact the Claude Code experience and should be taught as part of the first session.
proposals.rawActionItem: [UPDATE] Module 3 — Update Claude Code tool inventory and features: Add auto-plan mode (now automatic on substantial prompts), auto-memory learning mechanism, voice input (20 languages), `/claude-api` skill, MCP elicitation dialogs, `/effort` command, InstructionsLoaded hooks, ExitWorktree tool, and updated permission auto-approval allowlist

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection: Auto-Plan Mode and Structured Planning
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code automatically enters plan mode on substantial prompts, shifting toward spec-driven development. When you submit a complex or multi-step request, Claude Code pauses to think through the approach before writing code. This structured planning phase ensures careful feature design and reduces rework by aligning on the solution architecture first.
proposals.reason: Claude Code's 2026 auto-plan feature represents a significant shift in how developers interact with AI-assisted coding. This subsection documents the feature and explains its alignment with spec-driven development practices, filling a gap in Module 3's current coverage of the agentic loop.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Auto-Plan Mode and Structured Planning" subsection: Document how Claude Code automatically enters plan mode on substantial prompts and its shift toward spec-driven development

proposals.fieldLabel: New subsection: Auto-Memory and Learning from Errors
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code now includes Auto-Memory, a feature that enables Claude to learn from its own mistakes over time, improving accuracy and reducing repeated errors across sessions. This persistent memory mechanism tracks what went wrong, why it failed, and how to avoid similar issues in future interactions within the same project.
proposals.reason: Recent Claude Code feature updates (March 2026) introduced Auto-Memory as a core capability that fundamentally changes how Claude Code improves over time. This subsection documents this persistent memory mechanism for users learning Claude Code for the first time, ensuring they understand this learning capability from the start.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Auto-Memory and Learning from Errors" subsection: Document persistent memory mechanisms that improve accuracy and reduce repeated errors across sessions

proposals.fieldLabel: Voice-First Workflows with Claude Code subsection
proposals.proposedValue: Claude Code now supports voice-to-text input across 20+ languages, enabling hands-free coding and natural interaction. Voice Mode removes friction between ideas and prompts, making it ideal for accessibility, rapid prototyping, and developers who prefer speaking over typing. Simply enable Voice Mode in your Claude Code session and start speaking—Claude transcribes and processes your input in real time.
proposals.reason: Recent Claude Code updates (March 2026) introduced Voice Mode as a key feature that transforms developer workflows. Adding this subsection to Module 3 aligns the course with current product capabilities and highlights accessibility and productivity benefits that early learners should know about.
proposals.rawActionItem: [ADD] Module 3 — Add "Voice-First Workflows with Claude Code" subsection: Document voice-to-text input with 20-language support and accessibility benefits
